# tier_err — 197 samples extracted from ~/talkbank/data/*-data
# Each entry is separated by a blank line.
# Lines may include tab-continuations (multi-physical-line entries).

%err:	chrochro /CHOCHO/, leaves out the R after CH $PHO

%err:	chce /ŠCE/ $PHO, řepy = řepu fourth case $MOR, vnučku = uses the intonation as in six's case when calling someone $PHO

%err:	je = jsou ryby plural $MOR

%err:	oranžový = oranžová $MOR

%err:	jaká = barva or která, $LEX could have meant the word color = barva instead of the interrogative pronoun which = jaká, it could also be an attempt to use a relative pronoun

%err:	dubu = duha $LEX

%err:	piju = pije, third person sing . $MOR

%err:	piju = pije, tird person sing . $MOR; jaká = could be meant as an article "the" or the word "color" $LEX

%err:	sněh = sníh $LEX

%err:	je = jsou $MOR

%err:	stůl = na stole $MOR

%err:	stůl = židle $LEX, is an interference from german stuhl meaning chair

%err:	řepe /DŽepě/ $PHO

%err:	double verb, or attempt at gerundium which does not exist in czech

%err:	voda = vodu $MOR declination

%err:	double verb

%err:	sboříš /sboRíš/

%err:	plavat = paní plave $MOR

%err:	uses consciously the german preposition for "in" instead of the Czech "v"

%err:	consciously uses a german one instead of the czech one which he does not know

%err:	glóbusy = glóbus

%err:	zelenina = zelená $LEX

%err:	hnědů = hnědou $MOR $PHON,

%err:	bílů = bílá $MOR

%err:	strome = stromy in plural $MOR

%err:	pane = pan $MOR, uses the automatised vocative form instead of nominative because it is the form they use at the beginning of each class

%err:	overextends the rule about forming female family names Bialeková = Bialková $MOR

%err:	also overextends the above mentioned rule because the family name is unusual, of Polish origin
